title: Nasal polyp symptoms
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.
examination.rationale: The exact examination section supports clinician-performed nasal and throat examination including polyp observation when assessed; it does not infer histology or cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination

examination.text: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.
examination.rationale: The exact chronic-rhinosinusitis section supports documenting presence or absence of polyps, modifying factors, and existing imaging or specialist reports without ordering CT, endoscopy, or treatment.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 7A–10 — chronic distinction, objective confirmation, modifiers, testing, and polyps

medication.text: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.
medication.rationale: The exact rhinitis history section supports onset, obstruction, smell, discharge, sleep impact, asthma or allergy context, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without confirming a nasal polyp.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 7.1–7.10 — history, triggers, symptom pattern, impact, and contextual factors

source_limitations: Age, laterality, smell, obstruction, bleeding, asthma, aspirin sensitivity, examination, endoscopy, imaging, diagnosis, treatment, pathology, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
